mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS reflects an active tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend slope and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 in magnitude over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) versus flat-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 0.5 percentage point increase from its recent 6-month low, it signals an inflecting labor market that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, causing S&P 500 returns to be negative over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5pp from its trailing 6-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE snapshot at 4.7 percent as of t-1mo
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation combined with rising FEDFUNDS produces a negative real-rate squeeze narrative reversal: when nominal rates rise faster than inflation decelerates, short-horizon equity volatility increases and 5-day forward returns turn negative, but when inflation decelerates faster than rates rise, short-term relief rallies occur.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL momentum x FEDFUNDS momentum) shows Rank IC below 0.03 against 5-day forward returns over any 12-month rolling window, or sign of the interaction coefficient flips across more than half of quarterly subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in HOUST of more than 5% signals contracting housing demand sensitive to financing costs; this leading demand indicator predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during monetary tightening regimes, when the housing slowdown reflects genuine rate-driven demand destruction rather than seasonal noise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST snapshot 1090 thousand units at t-2mo
failure_condition: During tightening regimes, triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC < 0.03) when restricted to tightening-regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as it is incorporated into near-term earnings sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS snapshot 469092 million dollars at t-1mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth decelerates to below zero on a 3-month annualized basis, it signals an emerging industrial production contraction consistent with recession onset, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days) as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate falls below 0%
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following triggered INDPRO contraction are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in labor conditions, defined by PAYEMS month-over-month growth turning negative while UNRATE simultaneously rises by at least 0.3pp within a 3-month span (a Sahm-rule-like interaction), signals a labor market inflection that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change turns negative AND UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp within 3 months
regime_hint: labor market turning point / early recession signal
failure_condition: Triggered joint-condition episodes show non-negative average 20-day forward SPX returns, or the interaction Rank IC is not meaningfully higher (by more than 0.02) than either single-series Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 5%, the market prices in an extended tightening path, causing S&P 500 quarterly (60-day) forward returns to be negative; conversely, once inflation decelerates below 5% while FEDFUNDS plateaus, forward returns turn positive as tightening-cycle-end expectations build.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 5% while FEDFUNDS is on a rising trend
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during triggered high-inflation tightening periods are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the direction of the effect reverses once tested against a matched sample of prior tightening cycles.
